title: Help me know what to do right now
intro: Answer what is true. This does not replace 911, 988, poison control, medical care, or law enforcement.

items.notBreathing: They are not breathing, unconscious, blue/gray, or cannot be woken up.
items.suicide: They are threatening suicide or serious self-harm.
items.violence: They are threatening violence or someone may get hurt.
items.weapons: Weapons are involved or weapon access is a concern.
items.drivingIntoxicated: They are trying to drive while intoxicated.
items.childrenPresent: Children are present or may be exposed to the crisis.
items.missing: They are missing or no one knows where they are.
items.aggressive: They are verbally aggressive, escalating, or impossible to reason with.
items.askingMoney: They are pressuring the family for money, housing, keys, or a rescue.
items.willingTalk: They are sober enough and willing to talk calmly.

RED.title: Immediate safety risk
RED.body: This is not a coaching conversation. Move toward safety and contact emergency services now.
RED.action: Call 911 or 988 now if there is immediate medical danger, violence, or suicide risk.

ORANGE.title: Active crisis risk
ORANGE.body: Do not argue, debate treatment, or try to control the situation alone. Focus on safety, children, keys, distance, and calm next steps.
ORANGE.action: Use the checklist below, then use Urgent Text Line or request Premium video support when safe.

YELLOW.title: Escalating concern
YELLOW.body: The pattern needs a clear family response. This is a good time to set a boundary, document what happened, and plan before the next escalation.
YELLOW.action: Build a boundary and update the incident log/safety plan.

GREEN.title: Stable concern
GREEN.body: There may not be immediate danger, but the family should still plan, align, and stop enabling patterns early.
GREEN.action: Use education, readiness, and boundary tools before things escalate.

checklist.title: 2. Checklist
redOverdose: Call 911 now. Say overdose/poisoning is suspected if that is true.
Give naloxone if available. Repeat per package instructions if needed.
Start rescue breathing/CPR if trained and safe to do so.
Put them in recovery position if breathing but unconscious.
Stay until responders arrive. Tell responders what substances may be involved.
redSuicide: Call or text 988 now. Call 911 if there is immediate danger or means are present.
Do not leave them alone if it is safe for you to stay nearby.
Remove access to weapons, pills, or keys only if you can do it safely.
Use short, calm statements. Do not debate, shame, or threaten.
Bring in another safe adult immediately if possible.
redDefault: Leave the area if violence/weapons are possible. Physical safety comes first.
Move children away from the scene immediately if you can do so safely.
Do not physically block, restrain, or chase an intoxicated person.
Document threats after everyone is safe.
orange: Stop arguing. No treatment debate while they are intoxicated or escalated.
Move children, keys, cash, and medications to safety if you can do so calmly.
Do not give money, rides to unsafe places, or access to the car.
Use one short script. Repeat it. Do not over-explain.
Log the incident once the immediate situation settles.
default: Name the pattern in writing without exaggerating or minimizing.
Choose one boundary the family can actually keep.
Decide who communicates. Too many voices creates chaos.
Prepare treatment/support options before making big threats.
Ask: am I helping recovery or reducing my own anxiety?

say.title: 3. What to say right now
say.red: “I love you. Right now this is about safety. I’m calling for emergency help.”
say.money: “I love you too much to give money while addiction is active. I will help you get real support, but I will not fund the addiction.”
say.escalated: “I’m not going to argue while you’re intoxicated or escalated. I’ll talk when we can both stay safe and respectful.”
say.willing: “I love you. I’m willing to talk about real help and next steps. I’m not willing to keep pretending this is working.”
say.default: “I love you, and I’m changing how I respond. I will support recovery. I will not support the addiction.”

incident.title: 5. Incident log
incident.alertBody: Add a short summary before saving.

builder.title: 6. Boundary builder
builder.support: Support you are willing to offer
builder.noLongerDo: What you will no longer do

defaults.noLongerDo: give money, cover up consequences, or argue with addiction
defaults.consequence: I will step back, protect my peace, and contact help if safety becomes a concern

builder.template: I love you and I want you healthy. When {{behavior}}, I am not going to keep responding the old way. I will support {{support}}. I will no longer {{noLongerDo}}. If the boundary is crossed, {{consequence}}.

videoAlerts.gateBody: Private video support is included with Premium. Upgrade from the Support tab.
videoAlerts.queuedBody: Your private video support request is in the admin queue. This is not an emergency service.

share.title: Sober Helpline Crisis Summary
share.heading: Sober Helpline Family Crisis Summary
share.note: Note: This is family guidance, not medical, legal, or emergency-service replacement. Call 911/988 for immediate danger.

video.waitingNote: This is not an emergency service. Call 911 or 988 for immediate danger.
video.webOnly: Private video sessions are available in the iOS app.
